name: Leshnikovtsi
description: village in Tran municipality, Pernik oblast, Bulgaria

name: Mramor
description: village in Tran municipality, Pernik oblast, Bulgaria

name: Bogoyna
description: village in Tran municipality, Pernik oblast, Bulgaria

name: Toros
description: village in Lukovit municipality, Lovech oblast, Bulgaria

name: Dermantsi
description: village in Lukovit municipality, Lovech oblast, Bulgaria

name: Daben
description: village in Lukovit municipality, Lovech oblast, Bulgaria

name: Busmantsi
description: village in Stolichna municipality, Sofia Capital oblast, Bulgaria

name: Brestnitsa
description: village in Yablanitsa municipality, Lovech oblast, Bulgaria

name: Voneshta voda
description: village in Tryavna municipality, Gabrovo oblast, Bulgaria

name: Banite
description: village in Madan municipality, Smolyan oblast, Bulgaria

name: Georgi Damyanovo
description: village in Georgi Damyanovo municipality, Montana oblast, Bulgaria

name: Nikola Kozlevo
description: village in Nikola Kozlevo municipality, Shumen oblast, Bulgaria

name: Gorna Malina
description: village in Gorna Malina municipality, Sofia oblast, Bulgaria

name: Avren
description: village in Avren municipality, Varna oblast, Bulgaria

name: Stambolovo
description: village in Stambolovo municipality, Haskovo oblast, Bulgaria

name: Lesichovo
description: village in Lesichovo municipality, Pazardzhik oblast, Bulgaria

name: Haghpat
description: village in Lori Province of Armenia

name: Gorhavan
description: village in Ararat Province of Armenia

name: Haghartsin
description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

name: T`eghowt
description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

name: Ptghavan
description: village in Lori Province of Armenia

description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

name: Baghanis
description: village in Tavush Province of Armenia

name: shnnaayr
description: human settlement

name: Nivice
description: Stone-built Kurvelesh mountain village with transhumance routes and oral polyphonic traditions.

name: Progonat
description: Remote highland settlement known for dry-stone terraces, cedar smoke kitchens, and seasonal flocks.

name: Lekdush
description: Small inland hamlet where villagers preserve chestnut drying and hand-built irrigation channels.

name: Borsh i Siperm
description: Upper Borsh hillside quarter with olive terraces, old mills, and less-visited family farm routes.

name: Buben
description: Low-profile village near old floodplain farms, known for reed craft and riverbank seed saving.

name: Shirq
description: Historic riverside settlement around old abbey ruins, with fishing knots and net-mending knowledge.

name: Zogaj
description: Lake-edge village with chestnut smokehouses, wooden boat upkeep, and borderland culinary memory.

name: Pangri Zampa
description: Nestled just outside Thimphu city, Pangri Zampa is home to a significant historic monastery and a renowned astrology school, offering a deep insight into Bhutanese spiritual and traditional education.

name: Talo
description: Perched on a plateau overlooking the Punakha Valley, Talo is famed for its stunning terraced fields, pristine natural beauty, and its strong historical connections to the Bhutanese royal family.

name: Lobesa
description: A vibrant village in the Punakha Valley, Lobesa is widely known for Chimi Lhakhang, the 'fertility temple', and its picturesque rice paddies that dominate the landscape.

name: Shapa
description: Located in the picturesque Paro Valley, Shapa embodies the authentic rural Bhutanese lifestyle with its traditional farmhouses, vast agricultural lands, and serene, unspoiled natural environment.

name: Gaselo
description: A tranquil and remote village in Wangdue Phodrang, Gaselo is characterized by its productive high-altitude terraced fields and offers panoramic views of the surrounding majestic mountains.

name: Bang Nam Phueng
description: A tranquil village within Bangkok's 'Green Lung,' known for its vibrant weekend floating market. It offers local foods, crafts, and a glimpse into a traditional Thai riverside lifestyle preserved despite its proximity to the capital.

name: Ko Kret
description: A unique car-free island in the Chao Phraya River, preserving the culture of the Mon people. It is renowned for its distinctive Mon pottery, ancient temples, and a peaceful, slow-paced atmosphere.

name: Amphawa
description: A charming riverside community famous for its traditional wooden houses and an enchanting evening floating market. Life here revolves around the Mae Klong River, with locals engaged in fruit farming and preparing traditional Thai sweets.
traditions: Canal boat tours to see fireflies

name: Don Hoi Lot
description: This unique coastal village is famous for its distinctive mudflats teeming with razor clams (hoi lot), which are harvested by local fishermen using traditional methods. It offers an authentic glimpse into a livelihood deeply connected to the sea and its rich bounty.

name: Ban Mai Market
description: A century-old market village nestled along the Bang Pakong River, preserving classic wooden shophouses and a nostalgic atmosphere. It's a culinary haven known for its traditional Thai sweets, snacks, and a glimpse into a bygone era of riverside trade.

name: Sitges
description: A vibrant coastal town known for its beautiful beaches, artistic heritage, and lively carnival. Its charming old town is filled with narrow streets, boutique shops, and excellent restaurants.

name: Rupit
description: A stunning medieval village perched on a rocky outcrop, famous for its cobblestone streets, stone houses, and a picturesque wooden suspension bridge. It feels like stepping back in time.

name: Mura
description: Tucked away in the Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park, Mura is a beautifully preserved medieval village with narrow alleys, ancient houses, and a charming stream running through it.

name: Cardona
description: Home to one of Catalonia's most important medieval fortresses and a unique salt mountain, Cardona offers a deep dive into history and geology. Its historic center is rich with ancient buildings and stories.
traditions: Festa Major de la Sal (Salt Festival)

name: Cadaqués
description: A quintessential whitewashed fishing village on the Costa Brava, renowned for its bohemian charm and as a former retreat for artists like Salvador Dalí. Its unique beauty is framed by the Cap de Creus natural park.

name: Bell Buckle
description: A charming historic village known for its well-preserved Victorian architecture, unique antique shops, and the famous annual RC-MoonPie Festival. It offers a quaint, artistic atmosphere with a strong sense of community and heritage.

name: Lynchburg
description: A small, dry town famous worldwide as the home of the Jack Daniel's Distillery, offering a glimpse into traditional Southern life and whiskey-making heritage. Despite its fame, it maintains a quiet, rural village charm with a historic town square.
traditions: Community gatherings in the town square

name: Leiper's Fork
description: This historic village is celebrated for its commitment to preserving its 19th-century character, attracting artists, musicians, and visitors seeking a tranquil rural escape. It's renowned for its unique art galleries, antique shops, and vibrant local music scene.

name: Wartrace
description: A quaint historic railroad village featuring charming Victorian homes and a rich past connected to the Tennessee Walking Horse. Its quiet streets and well-preserved architecture offer a peaceful step back in time.

name: Flat Creek
description: A small, unincorporated rural community nestled along Flat Creek, characterized by its agricultural roots and quiet country living. It represents a quintessential piece of rural Tennessee life, focused on farming and local community ties.

name: Cavtat
description: A picturesque coastal town built on the site of ancient Epidaurum, known for its charming harbor, historical villas, and the Racic Mausoleum. Its rich history and beautiful seafront promenades offer a blend of cultural heritage and relaxed Mediterranean charm.

name: Mali Ston
description: Famous for its medieval walls, which are part of the 'European Great Wall' connecting it to Ston, and its world-renowned oyster farms. The village thrives on aquaculture, offering fresh seafood in a tranquil historical setting.

name: Cilipi
description: The heart of Konavle folklore, this village is celebrated for its vibrant Sunday morning performances of traditional dances and music in authentic costumes. It offers an immersive experience into the region's rich cultural heritage and artisanal crafts.

name: Trsteno
description: Home to the historic Trsteno Arboretum, a stunning Renaissance garden featuring exotic plants, ancient trees, and Baroque fountains. This peaceful village offers a unique blend of botanical beauty and historical architecture.

name: Slano
description: Nestled in a deep, picturesque bay, Slano is a peaceful retreat with a rich history, evident in its Franciscan monastery and ruins of a ducal palace. It's known for its clear waters, natural beauty, and a relaxed Mediterranean atmosphere.

name: Pella
description: Known as the birthplace of Alexander the Great, Pella boasts significant archaeological ruins including a large ancient city and museum, offering a deep dive into ancient Macedonian history and culture. The modern village thrives amidst this rich historical backdrop.

name: Vergina
description: Home to the Royal Tombs of Aigai, a UNESCO World Heritage site, Vergina is where Philip II of Macedon (Alexander's father) was buried. It's a place of immense historical importance, preserving the grandeur of ancient Macedonian royalty.

name: Afytos
description: Perched on a cliff overlooking the Toroneos Gulf in Halkidiki, Afytos is a picturesque village renowned for its traditional stone architecture, narrow cobbled streets, and breathtaking sea views. It combines historic charm with a vibrant artistic community.

name: Arnaia
description: A charming mountain village in Halkidiki, Arnaia is famous for its well-preserved Macedonian architecture with colorful two-story houses and cobblestone squares. It's a hub for traditional crafts and offers a glimpse into rural Greek life.

name: Palaios Panteleimonas
description: This wonderfully preserved traditional village, nestled on the slopes of Mount Olympus, offers panoramic views of the Thermaic Gulf. With its stone houses, quaint squares, and absence of cars, it's a peaceful retreat embodying old-world charm.

name: Kondovo
region: Gjorče Petrov Municipality, Skopje Statistical Region
description: Kondovo is a predominantly Albanian village known for its traditional rural lifestyle and strong community bonds. It offers a glimpse into the everyday life and customs of the local Albanian population.

name: Mirkovci
description: Nestled in the foothills of Skopska Crna Gora, Mirkovci is a traditional Macedonian village preserving old customs and architectural styles. Its serene environment and close-knit community reflect a deep connection to its historical roots.

name: Banjani
description: Banjani is a picturesque village situated in the Skopska Crna Gora region, known for its beautiful nature and rich Orthodox Christian heritage. The village maintains a strong sense of community and traditional values.

name: Marino
description: Marino is an agricultural village east of Skopje, characterized by its fertile lands and a mix of ethnic communities. It serves as a local hub for farming and traditional crafts, reflecting its agrarian lifestyle.

name: Katlanovo
description: Katlanovo is renowned for its natural thermal springs and spa, offering a unique blend of wellness tourism and traditional village life. The surrounding area is rich in natural beauty and historical significance.

name: Rewa
description: Rewa is a historically significant chiefly village, home to the Roko Tui Dreketi, one of Fiji's paramount chiefs. It lies on the Rewa River, embodying a deep connection to Fijian traditional governance and history.

name: Burebasaga
description: Another important chiefly village, Burebasaga is central to the Burebasaga Confederacy and known for its strong communal ties. It maintains ancient customs and is a significant cultural hub in the Rewa delta.

name: Nasilai
description: Nasilai is a coastal village known for its strong connection to the sea and traditional fishing practices. It boasts a vibrant maritime culture and a community deeply intertwined with its surrounding marine environment.

name: Namosi
description: Nestled in the rugged interior of Viti Levu, Namosi Village is the heart of the Namosi Province, preserving a unique highland Fijian culture. Its remote location contributes to its strong traditional customs and pristine natural surroundings.

name: Wainibokasi
description: Located along the Wainibokasi River, this village is a hub for riverine communities, known for its fertile lands and agricultural practices. It reflects a traditional Fijian way of life closely tied to the river and land.

name: Lukomir
description: Highest permanently inhabited village in Bosnia. Stone houses, flat-roofed and unchanged since the Ottoman era. No road in winter.

name: Umoljani
description: Medieval necropolis village beneath Bjelašnica. Stećak tombstones scatter the meadows between timber houses.

name: Vrelo
description: Spring-fed village along the Vrbas river canyon. Trout fishing and water-mill milling have never stopped here.

name: Studenci
description: Karst plateau village above Mostar. Dry-stone walls divide vineyards producing Herzegovina's oldest wine grape.

name: Blagaj
description: Dervish tekke carved into the rock above the Buna spring. The spring produces 43 cubic metres per second from solid limestone.

name: Gostuša
description: Stone village clinging to a cliff above the Visočica river. Abandoned in winter, revived each spring by the same dozen families.

name: Rajac
description: Village famous for its underground wine cellars – bačije – carved into the hillside. Each family maintains one, some over 200 years old.

name: Zlot
description: Gateway to the Lazareva cave system. The village has guided cave expeditions since 1888 and keeps the oldest karst logbook in the Balkans.

name: Sirogojno
description: Open-air museum village preserving 19th-century Zlatibor homesteads. Every building in use. Knitting cooperative founded 1962 still exports worldwide.

name: Mokra Gora
description: Terminus of the Šargan Eight narrow-gauge railway. Village of wooden houses and dense pine forest above the Drina canyon.

name: Gornja Lastva
description: Stone village above the Bay of Kotor visible from across the water. Olive groves planted in the 14th century still produce.

name: Rijeka Crnojevića
description: First printing press in the Balkans was here in 1493. The river runs turquoise through a limestone gorge to Lake Skadar.

name: Žabljak Crnojevića
description: Abandoned medieval capital on a peninsula in Lake Skadar. The fortress walls are still standing; the village inside is half-empty.

name: Kovači
description: Mountain plateau village above Nikšić. Pasture land used in common since the 13th century, cattle rotated by village council vote.

name: Dobrsko Selo
description: Village above the Morača canyon with a church frescoed by the monks of Morača monastery in 1252. Fewer than 40 residents remain.

name: Kryvorivnia
description: Hutsul cultural heartland in the Carpathian valley. Mykhailo Kotsiubynsky wrote Shadows of Forgotten Ancestors here. Embroidered shirts still worn on Sundays.

name: Busha
description: Rock-carved hermit church from the 11th century. The plateau above the Southern Buh river is covered in ancient burial mounds.

name: Sheshory
description: Annual ethno-music festival village in the Pistynka valley. Outside festival season it reverts to a stream-side cheese-making settlement.

name: Mezhyhiria
description: Village beside the ancient Mezhyhirsky monastery on the Dnipro bluffs. Potters have worked the local white clay since the 13th century.

name: Verkhovyna
description: Administrative centre of the Hutsul highland. Every spring the Hutsul Games are held here — wrestling, axe-throwing, horse skills.

name: Yörük Köyü
description: A Yörük nomad settlement village near Safranbolu that stopped migrating three generations ago. Original black felt tent panels are stored in every house.

name: Çavuşin
description: The old quarter of Çavuşin is entirely carved into tufa cliffs abandoned after a rockfall in 1963. The new village watches over it from across the valley.

name: Halfeti
description: Partially submerged village on the Euphrates. The black rose grows only here and in one location in Yemen. Upper Halfeti was relocated; lower walls still visible at low water.

name: Birgi
description: Finest concentration of Ottoman civilian timber houses in Turkey. The Çakırağa Mansion is entirely original with hand-painted walls.

name: Uzungöl
description: Glacial lake village in the Kaçkar foothills. The mosque reflects in the water at dawn. Tea gardens have grown uninterrupted on the slopes since 1850.

name: Dana
description: Ottoman-era stone village perched at the rim of Wadi Dana. One of Jordan's last inhabited cliff villages, it descends 1500m to the Rift Valley.

name: Beidha
description: Little Petra's village, adjacent to the Neolithic site of Ain Ghazal. Farmers still cultivate the same terraces used 9000 years ago.

name: Umm Qais Village
description: Village built within the ruins of ancient Gadara. Basalt-paved streets, Roman theatre used for village meetings, fig trees growing from column bases.

name: Iraq al-Amir
description: Village of the Tobiad family mansion carved from a single rock. Women's cooperative produces papyrus, ceramics and textile replicas since 1997.

name: Rum Village
description: The only permanent settlement in Wadi Rum. Zalabia Bedouin still live in goat-hair tents in the valley; the village is their dry-season base.

name: Ghandruk
description: Gurung heartland village with full views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre. The Gurung Museum preserves original kukri, jewellery and shamanistic ritual objects.

name: Bandipur
description: Newar trading town frozen in the 18th century after a new road bypassed it. The main street is car-free. Every building is original Newari timber-brick.

name: Kagbeni
description: Walled medieval town at the entrance to Upper Mustang. Adobe houses painted ochre. The monastery has a 500-year-old tree in its courtyard.

name: Tansen
description: Hilltop Newari bazaar town known for dhaka textile and metalwork. The Tansen durbar square is intact. Dhaka weavers work on backstrap looms in their ground floors.

name: Marpha
description: Apple orchard village in the rain shadow of the Himalayas. White-walled lanes, covered passages, and apple brandy distilleries operating since the 1960s.

name: Ban Xang Khong
description: Weaving village across the Mekong from Luang Prabang. Every household has a loom. Silk and cotton patterns memorised without diagrams across generations.

name: Ban Phanom
description: Tai Lue weaving village whose textile museum opened in a restored house in 2003. The matriarchs remember patterns from before the revolution.

name: Muang Ngoi Neua
description: Car-free village on the Nam Ou river accessible only by boat. Limestone karst towers on all sides. No road. Generator powers lights 6pm to 10pm.

name: Pha Tang
description: Akha hill-tribe village near the Chinese border. Distinctive headdresses worn daily, not for tourists. Coffee grown under forest canopy since 1940.

name: Don Khon
description: River island in the 4000 Islands where the Mekong splits. Irrawaddy dolphins still feed in the rapids. French railway track from 1897 still crosses the island.

name: Duong Lam
description: First village awarded National Relic status in Vietnam. Laterite stone houses unchanged for 400 years, two ancient communal houses, and the birthplace of two Vietnamese kings.

name: Lac Village
description: White Thai stilt-house village in Mai Chau valley. Silk looms under every house. The valley floor is one continuous rice terrace lit gold before harvest.

name: Cự Đà
description: Vermicelli and soy sauce village on the Nhuệ river. The French colonial shophouses haven't been repainted since 1954. Every household produces bún tàu.

name: Phước Tích
description: Ancient pottery village on the Ô Lâu river. Ceramics supplied the royal court of Hue for 500 years. Only 300 people remain; most are over 60.

name: Y Linh Hồ
description: Black H'Mong village in a valley below Sapa, with terraced fields carved over 300 years. The village has refused electricity grid connection to maintain its quiet.
traditions: new rice festival xin lúa mới

name: Aït Ben Haddou
description: UNESCO ksar of earthen clay towers on the Ounila river. Only a handful of families still live inside the walls; the rest crossed to the new village in the 1990s.

name: Moulay Idriss Zerhoun
description: Morocco's holiest town, burial place of Moulay Idriss I. Until 1912 non-Muslims were forbidden to stay the night. White cylindrical minaret unique in Morocco.

name: Aït Mansour Gorge Village
description: Palm-shaded gorge village in the Anti-Atlas. The gorge walls are 300m high and the trail passes through three distinct micro-climates in two hours.

name: Ain Tissimane
description: Shepherd village above Chefchaouen in the cedar forest. Goats outnumber people 20 to 1. Fresh goat milk cheese sold from doorways every morning.

name: Khamlia
description: Gnawa music village on the edge of Erg Chebbi dunes. The entire village descends from sub-Saharan freed slaves. Gnawa healing ceremonies held every Friday night.

name: Fadiouth
description: Island village built entirely on a midden of clam shells. The cemetery mixes Muslim and Christian graves. The granary island is reached by a wooden bridge.

name: Touba Village
description: Founding settlement of Mouridiyya Sufi brotherhood, separate from the city of Touba. The original Cheikh Amadou Bamba homestead is here.

name: Wassadou
description: Gambia River lodge village at the edge of Niokolo-Koba park. Hippos surface 10 metres from the bank. Village fishermen and park rangers are the same men.

name: Bétenty
description: Diola village on a delta creek in Casamance. The sacred forest is off-limits to outsiders but the elders allow drum and dance ceremony observation during harvest.

name: Popenguine
description: Catholic pilgrimage village on a red laterite cliff above the Atlantic. The annual Pentecost pilgrimage draws 50,000 walkers. Between pilgrimages the village fishes.

name: Chinchero
description: Inca market town at 3762m above the Sacred Valley. The original Inca stone walls support the Spanish colonial church built directly on top. Weavers still use backstrap looms in the plaza.

name: Ollantaytambo Village
description: The only surviving Inca urban grid still inhabited. Canchas — original communal blocks — still function. The fortress above has never been fully excavated.

name: Taquile
description: Island in Lake Titicaca where the men knit and the women spin. UNESCO heritage of Taquile textile art. No hotels — only family homestays.

name: Pisac
description: Inca citadel village with the largest Inca terracing system still irrigated. Sunday market has operated since Inca times. The cemetery above has 10,000 chullpas.

name: Maras
description: Pre-Inca salt pans at 3380m fed by a salt-saturated spring. Over 3000 evaporation pools owned by individual families. Salt has been harvested here for 800 years.

name: Atyrá
description: Paraguay's lace-making capital. Ñandutí spider-web lace has been produced here since the 18th century. Each motif is unique and passed between women of the same family.

name: San Cosme y Damián
description: Jesuit reduction with the best-preserved astronomical observatory in South America, built by indigenous craftsmen in 1760. The gnomon still casts accurate noon shadows.

name: Colonia Independencia
description: German-Guaraní hybrid village in the Ybytyruzú hills. The wine cooperative founded in 1919 produces Paraguay's only certified wine. German surnames on Guaraní land titles.

name: Altos
description: Hilltop town above Asunción with the oldest established ceramics tradition in Paraguay. Red clay pots fired in the same design as the 1500s mission records.

name: Tobatí
description: Wood-carving capital of Paraguay. Santera religious figure carving has been produced for the Jesuit missions since the 1600s. Carpenters still use blades made from recycled car springs.

name: Teotitlán del Valle
description: Zapotec weaving village where every family produces wool rugs using cochineal, indigo and marigold dyes unchanged since before the Spanish. The church is built on top of the pyramid.

name: Real de Catorce
description: Silver mining ghost town at 2750m, accessible only through a 2.3km lit tunnel. The Wixáritari (Huichol) consider the desert around it their most sacred peyote pilgrimage site.

name: San Juan Chamula
description: Tzotzil Maya municipality that expelled the Catholic priest in 1867 and runs its own syncretist church. Photography prohibited inside. Pine needles, incense, and Coca-Cola offerings.

name: Cuetzalan del Progreso
description: Cloud-forest Totonac village in the coffee and vanilla belt. Voladores ceremony — four men spiralling from a 30m pole — performed on Sunday mornings.

name: Cocucho
description: Purépecha pottery village producing the famous ugly ceramic devil figures — diablitos. Every household is a kiln. The clay comes from a single hillside quarry shared by the village.

name: Baddeck
description: Cape Breton village on Bras d'Or Lake where Alexander Graham Bell spent his summers. The Gaelic College 5km away keeps the only Scottish Gaelic immersion school in North America.

name: St. Martins
description: Bay of Fundy village with the world's highest tides. The two covered bridges span a river that is dry at low tide. Sea caves are only accessible for 90 minutes around low water.

name: Carcross
description: Carcross/Tagish First Nation village at the junction of Nares Lake and Bennett Lake. The world's smallest desert — 2.6km² of shifting sand dunes — is 1km away.

name: Barkerville
description: Gold rush town of 1862 restored to full operation. Actors in period dress. The only working assay office and saloon in original condition in Canada.

name: Chéticamp
description: Acadian French fishing village at the entrance to Cape Breton Highlands. The hooked rug cooperative founded in 1968 exports worldwide. Mass is still sung in Acadian French.

name: Simbai
description: Kalam-speaking highland village accessible only by small aircraft or three-day walk. The Kalam people have 170 named bird species in their oral taxonomy with no written language.

name: Tufi
description: Fjord village cut into the Tufi cape by a drowned river system. The Orokaiva tribe has practiced tattooing and scarification here for centuries. The longest fjord is 18km.

name: Asaro
description: Home of the Asaro Mudmen, who wear white clay masks in battle tradition. The clay comes from a single riverbed. Modern performances are in original ritual context at the village, not for stages.

name: Alexishafen
description: German colonial mission village on Madang Bay. The original 1896 church is intact. Canoe races held on the feast of the Assumption have mixed with local Madang spirit ceremony.

name: Omarakana
description: Traditional capital of the Kiriwina chief on the Trobriand Islands. Yam competition — who grows the largest — determines social prestige. The yam house facades are decorated by the chief's status.
